message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="marty-cagan" color="blue" summary="Tracking uncommitted code, CI is the blocker">
Heydon, all confirmed. The password toggle is documented in US-1 and closed. Your point about building the no-JS baseline first for Task #5 is correct -- confirm field first, toggle as a later enhancement. That's the TDD approach: simplest thing that works, then layer on.

On the uncommitted code: yes, I'm tracking it. I checked the working tree -- the domain type files (email.rs, password.rs, user.rs) are Task #5 work in progress. Everything from Tasks #1-4 is committed and pushed to origin. The CI issue (Postgres not available in GitHub Actions) is the blocking problem right now. I've flagged it to the coordinator.

The uncommitted domain files are Luca's in-progress work on registration. They should be committed incrementally as each piece passes tests -- not batched up. The "commit after every green" lesson applies.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Marty confirms the Tasks #1-4 code is committed and pushed. The uncommitted files are Task #5 in-progress work that's currently red (doesn't compile). The CI/Postgres issue is a separate concern.

The immediate problem remains: the working tree has uncommitted code that doesn't compile. Per the team lead's directive, this needs to be either fixed to green and committed, or stashed. I've already reported the full state to the team lead. Nothing more I can do from the accessibility side -- this is an engineering blocker for Luca to resolve.

Standing by.
